titleStmt.title: Copper plates from Kondavidu (set I) βÄî reign of PrΧΞthivΡΪ≈¦rΡΪm≈Ϊla, year 25

titleStmt.title: Copper plates from Kondavidu (set I) βÄî reign of PrΧΞthivΡΪ≈¦rΡΪm≈Ϊla, year 25

respStmt.resp: assistance with XML encoding and metadata verification
assistance with XML encoding and metadata verification

editionStmt.p: First digital edition made by Οâcole franΟßaise d'ExtrΟΣme-Orient (Paris, France), realized in collaboration with the HiSoMA Research Centre (Lyon, France) and hosted by TGIR Huma-Num (France) as Early Inscriptions of ΡÄndhrade≈¦a, in 2015-2017.

licence.p: Copyright (c) 2017 by Arlo Griffiths and Vincent Tournier.

This work is licensed under the Creative Commons Attribution 3.0 Unported License. To view a copy of this license, visit http://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/3.0/ or send a letter to Creative Commons, 444 Castro Street, Suite 900, Mountain View, California, 94041, USA.

projectDesc.p: The project DHARMA has received funding from the European Research Council (ERC) under the European Union's Horizon 2020 research and innovation programme (grant agreement no 809994).

prefixDef.p: Public URIs with the prefix bib to point to a Zotero Group Library named ERC-DHARMA whose data are open to the public.
Internal URIs using the part prefix to point to person elements in the DHARMA_IdListMembers.xml file.

revisionDesc.change: EIAD file transformed to follow the DHARMA encoding structure. Metadata extracted to be checked and updated according DHARMA workflow. Done through XSLT.

div.ab:
1
svasti vijayatΡ¹αΙ΅αΗçikoαΙ΅αΗçavΡ¹sakΡ¹d devadvijagurucaraαΙ΅asamΡ¹rΡ¹dhinΡ¹
dhigatanirati≈¦ayapuαΙ΅yanicayasya sakaladiαΙÖmaαΙ΅αΗçalΡ¹laαΙÖkΡ¹rabh≈Ϊtaya
≈¦asaαΗΞ ≈¦rΡΪprabhΡ¹karavikhyΡ¹tamahΡ¹rΡ¹jasya s≈Ϊnur mmΡ¹tΡ¹pitrΧΞpΡ¹dΡ¹nuddhyΡ¹ta≈¦ ≈¦ruti
smrΧΞtivihitapadΡ¹rtthΡ¹vabodhajanitaprajΟ±Ρ¹vivekaniravagΡΪtΡ¹nupΡ¹litΡ¹
≈¦eαΙΘavarαΙ΅αΙ΅Ρ¹≈¦ramadharmmaαΗΞ parΡ¹nugrahamΡ¹traprayojana
pratipannai≈¦varyagurubhΡ¹raαΗΞ paramamΡ¹he≈¦varaαΗΞ
≈¦rΡΪmΡ¹n prΧΞthivΡΪ≈¦rΡΪm≈ΪlarΡ¹ja Evam Ρ¹jΟ±Ρ¹payati yathΡ¹
ya tΡ¹αΗΜupΡ¹kaviαΙΘaye velpukoαΙ΅αΗça≈ΣαΙüukoαΙ΅αΗçamaddi
2
ceruvuΡÄtuk≈ΪrΡ¹khyagrΡ¹mamaddhyΡ¹vasthitaαΗΞ kalvaceαΙüuvuαΗΖa
nΡ¹ma grΡ¹maαΗΞ ≈¦rΡΪmahΡ¹meghavΡ¹hanapratiαΙΘαΙ≠hΡ¹pitavarddhamΡ¹
nimahΡ¹vihΡ¹ranivΡ¹sarater ddhyΡ¹nΡ¹dhyayanakarmmΡ¹nuαΙΘαΙ≠hΡ¹na
parasyΡ¹ryyabhikαΙΘusaαΙ¹ghasya yathΡ¹sukhaαΙ¹ caturvvidhapratyaya
paribhogΡ¹ya sarvvΡ¹bΡ¹dhaparihΡ¹reαΙ΅a sarvvΡ¹dΡ¹naparivΡ¹raαΗΞ
tΡ¹mra≈¦Ρ¹sanΡΪkrΧΞtya mahΡ¹mΡ¹trapuαΙ΅ya≈¦Ρ¹sanamΡ¹tΡ¹mahΡΪ
puαΙ΅yaprΡ¹ptyartthaαΙ¹ prΧΞdhivΡΪ≈¦rΡΪm≈ΪlarΡ¹jena dattaαΗΞ tad atra
≈¦rutismrΧΞtivihitasadΡ¹cΡ¹rΡ¹nuαΙΘαΙ≠hΡ¹naparais sarvvair eva
3
rΡ¹jabhir ayam asmadΡΪyo dharmma Iti paripΡ¹lanΡΪyaαΗΞ
hastiko≈¦avΡΪrako≈¦ena ca tathΡ¹py uktaαΙ¹ .

ya≈¦ cΡ¹tra lobhapramΡ¹dΡ¹d ajΟ±Ρ¹nakαΙΘudraparivΡ¹ra
doαΙΘava≈¦Ρ¹d vΡ¹ vilopaαΙ¹ kuryΡ¹t¬Ζ tasyΡ¹yaαΙ¹ smrΧΞtivihitaαΗΞ
pratyavΡ¹yo yathΡ¹ha

pravarddhamΡ¹navijayasaαΙ¹vatsare paΟ±ca
viαΙ¹≈¦atitame ΡÄαΙΘaαΗçhamΡ¹sa≈¦uklapakαΙΘasya prati
padi svamukhΡ¹jΟ±Ρ¹

app.note: Emend yat. : (n.11) considers the akαΙΘara redundant.

corrects dha- to dhΡ¹-. But the reading -bΡ¹dhΡ¹pari- is not attested anywhere in these inscriptions. The reading as it stands could be corrected sarvvabΡ¹dhapari-, in which case one could argue that the scribe anticipated correct sarvvΡ¹- further on in the line. Note, however, that the same reading sarvvΡ¹bΡ¹dhaparihΡ¹reαΙ΅a is preserved in EIAD 175, l. 33; sarvvΡ¹bΡ¹dhaparihΡ¹ram is found in 170, l. 8. It therefore seems necessary to take the compound as sarvva + Ρ¹bΡ¹dha-.
corrects tra to trΡ¹, but this does not seem desirable.
Correct prΧΞthivΡΪ-. The scribe has omitted the central dot that would have made th out of dh. has not noticed the error.
The editor of Krishna SastryβÄôs publication (n. 15) proposes to correct to hastiko≈¦avΡΪrako≈¦Ρ¹bhyΡ¹αΙ¹.
Krishna Sastry corrects his reading to tathΡ¹py. It seems unproblematic to read th.
The final consonant looks a bit more like n¬Ζ, but it seems highly unlikely that this is what the engraver intended.

The reading is uncertain; one has the impression that the engraver first wrote αΙΘαΙ≠ and then tried to correct it into something which may as well have been intended to be ssth as sth. Emend -nabhasas sthitiαΗΞ, as indicated by .

div.p: Hail! From the victorious camp of TΡ¹αΙ΅αΗçikoαΙ΅αΗça, the illustrious king PrΧΞthivΡΪ≈¦rΡΪm≈Ϊla, blessed by the feet of his parents; upholder of all laws on class and stage of life, in impeccable manner, through [his] intelligence and discrimination produced by [his] perception of the meanings of the words enjoined in revelation and tradition; who had undertaken the heavy burden of sovereignty with the sole purpose of favoring others; devout worshiper of Mahe≈¦vara; son of the celebrated great king ≈örΡΪ PrabhΡ¹kara, whose fame was the ornament of all quarters of space, whose unsurpassed store of merit had been obtained by the veneration of the feet of gods, brahmins and masters βÄî he commands as follows:

βÄ‰The village named KalvaceαΙüuvuαΗΖ, situated in the middle of the villages called VelpukoαΙ΅αΗça, ≈ΣαΙüukoαΙ΅αΗça, Maddiceruvu and ΡÄtuk≈ΪrΡ¹, in the TΡ¹αΗΜupΡ¹ka district, has been given by king PrΧΞthivΡΪ≈¦rΡΪm≈Ϊla, accompanied by [the right to take] levies, with complete exemption from taxes, after making a charter in copper, with a view to his maternal grandmother obtaining the merit of a grant entailing very copious merit, for the purpose of unrestricted (yathΡ¹sukham) use for the fourfold requisites by the community of noble monks, devoted to the execution of tasks such as meditation and study, delighted to reside in the great monastery of VardhamΡ¹ni that had been established by ≈örΡΪ MahΡ¹meghavΡ¹hana βÄî therefore [it] is to be safeguarded by all [future] kings, devoted to the execution of good conduct in this connection, as enjoined in revelation and tradition, [thinking] βÄ€This is our duty,βÄù [and particularly] by the Hastiko≈¦a and the VΡΪrako≈¦a.βÄô And thus is it said:
By numerous [kings], land has been given; and by many it has been protected. Whoever holds land at a given moment, to him does the fruit then belong.

The one who would carry out the annulment [of a foundation], whether by cupidity and negligence, or by ignorance and under influence of the sins of lowly dependants, to fim [befalls] this offence stated in tradition. It is said:
The destiny of those who slay their master, a cow, a brahmin, a child, a woman or a learned man, will be the one of him who seizes [the land/livelihood] that is marked by a (royal) charter.
Hear the fruit of the virtuous man who protects it scrupulously: during numerous thousands of aeons he plays in heaven with the gods.
The giver of land revels in heaven for sixty thousand years; the one who seizes [land] as well as the one who approves (of the seizing) will reside as many [years] in hell.
Oral command [issued] in the twenty-fifth victorious and prosperous year, on the first [day] of the waxing fortnight of the month of ΡÄαΙΘΡ¹αΗçha.
As long as the endurance of oceans, Meru, sun, directions, earth and sky, so long may the charter (or: rule) of ≈örΡΪ M≈ΪlarΡ¹ja endure on earth!
First described and edited by : 72-3, 76-7. Re-edited here from available documentation and after autopsy of the plates.

listBibl.bibl: : 1-11. This reproduces the same authorβÄôs 1990 publication without any of the notes.
